attack_paths.name: Web Server Compromise
attack_paths.objective: Gain initial foothold on web servers

steps.action_description: Scan web servers for known vulnerabilities using public exploit databases and tools like Nuclei.
steps.outcome: Identified a critical vulnerability in the web server software.

mitigation.description: Update the web server software to the latest patched version.
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws_ssm_patch_manager

steps.action_description: Exploit the identified vulnerability to gain remote code execution on the web servers.
steps.outcome: Gained initial foothold on the web servers with privileged access.

mitigation.description: Update the web server software to the latest patched version.
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws_ssm_patch_manager

steps.action_description: Enumerate and abuse default credentials or overly permissive IAM roles associated with the web server instances.
steps.outcome: Obtained higher privileges and ability to access other resources like S3 buckets.

mitigation.description: Implement least privilege principles and review IAM roles and policies regularly.
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws_iam_policy_review

attack_paths.name: Data Exfiltration
attack_paths.objective: Steal sensitive user data

steps.action_description: Enumerate compromised web server to identify resources and data stores.
steps.outcome: Discovered S3 bucket 'user_documents' containing sensitive user data.

mitigation.description: Implement monitoring and auditing for data access and usage.
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws_cloudtrail, aws_config

steps.action_description: Access and download sensitive user data from the 'user_documents' S3 bucket.
steps.outcome: Exfiltrated sensitive user documents and files.

mitigation.description: Encrypt data at rest and in transit using strong encryption algorithms.
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws_kms, aws_s3_bucket_encryption

steps.action_description: Use a non-standard data transfer protocol to exfiltrate the stolen data.
steps.outcome: Sensitive user data successfully exfiltrated from the environment.

mitigation.description: Implement network segmentation and restrict outbound traffic to approved destinations.
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws_vpc_security_groups, aws_network_acls

attack_paths.name: Credential Abuse and Privilege Escalation
attack_paths.objective: Gain elevated privileges and access sensitive data

steps.action_description: Use compromised employee credentials to access AWS resources
steps.outcome: Gained initial access to AWS environment

mitigation.description: Centralize and manage asset inventory to mitigate risks from unauthorized assets.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Config to centrally manage and monitor AWS resources.

steps.action_description: Abuse the web server instance profile to escalate privileges
steps.outcome: Gained elevated privileges within the AWS environment

mitigation.description: Manage the creation of privileged accounts and correctly configure their permissions.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to manage and restrict access to privileged roles and resources.

steps.action_description: Access user session data stored in DynamoDB
steps.outcome: Obtained user session cookies and sensitive data

mitigation.description: Encrypt sensitive information at rest and in transit.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Key Management Service (KMS) to encrypt data in DynamoDB and other AWS services.

steps.action_description: Access and exfiltrate application API keys stored in AWS Secrets Manager
steps.outcome: Gained access to sensitive application data and APIs

mitigation.description: Monitor and audit access to cloud services and resources.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CloudTrail to monitor and audit access to AWS Secrets Manager and other services.

attack_paths.name: Data Exfiltration and Persistence
attack_paths.objective: Exfiltrate sensitive data and maintain access

steps.action_description: Use compromised employee credentials to access AWS resources
steps.outcome: Gained initial access to AWS environment

mitigation.description: Centralize and manage asset inventory to mitigate risks from unauthorized assets.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Config to centrally manage and monitor AWS resources.

steps.action_description: Access and exfiltrate sensitive user documents from S3 bucket
steps.outcome: Obtained sensitive user data and documents

mitigation.description: Monitor and audit access to sensitive data repositories.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CloudTrail to monitor and audit access to S3 buckets and other data repositories.

steps.action_description: Transfer exfiltrated data to a personal S3 bucket for persistence
steps.outcome: Maintained access to exfiltrated data and established persistence

mitigation.description: Monitor and audit access to cloud storage objects.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CloudTrail to monitor and audit access to S3 buckets and objects.

steps.action_description: Establish encrypted channel for remote command and control
steps.outcome: Gained persistent remote access and control

mitigation.description: Restrict outbound network traffic to authorized destinations only.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Network Firewall or Security Groups to restrict outbound network traffic.

attack_paths.name: Privilege Escalation and Data Exfiltration
attack_paths.objective: Gain administrative privileges and exfiltrate sensitive data

steps.action_description: Leverage legitimate access as a web server admin to enumerate permissions and identify potential privilege escalation paths.
steps.outcome: Identified overly permissive IAM policies attached to the web server role, allowing access to sensitive resources.

mitigation.description: Implement the principle of least privilege by granting only the necessary permissions required for a role or user to perform their intended tasks.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Review and tighten IAM policies, removing unnecessary permissions.

steps.action_description: Abuse the overly permissive S3 policy attached to the web server role to gain elevated privileges.
steps.outcome: Gained administrative access to the S3 bucket containing sensitive user documents.

mitigation.description: Separate privileges and permissions across different roles and accounts based on the principle of least privilege.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Create separate IAM roles and policies with distinct permissions for different application components and workloads.

steps.action_description: Leverage the elevated S3 privileges to access and exfiltrate sensitive user documents.
steps.outcome: Gained unauthorized access to sensitive user data stored in the S3 bucket.

mitigation.description: Encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit to protect against unauthorized access.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Enable server-side encryption for S3 buckets and enforce encryption in transit using HTTPS.

steps.action_description: Leverage the web server instance to exfiltrate the stolen data over HTTPS, disguising it as normal web traffic.
steps.outcome: Successfully exfiltrated sensitive user data outside the organization's network.

mitigation.description: Implement network monitoring and data loss prevention (DLP) solutions to detect and prevent unauthorized data exfiltration.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Deploy AWS Network Firewall or third-party DLP solutions to monitor and inspect network traffic for sensitive data.

attack_paths.name: Lateral Movement and Persistence
attack_paths.objective: Establish persistence and move laterally within the environment

steps.action_description: Analyze the permissions granted to the Lambda execution role and identify potential avenues for lateral movement.
steps.outcome: Discovered that the Lambda role has permissions to access other AWS services and resources.

mitigation.description: Implement the principle of least privilege by granting only the necessary permissions required for a role or user to perform their intended tasks.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Review and tighten IAM policies, removing unnecessary permissions.

steps.action_description: Modify the Lambda function code to include malicious logic that corrupts or deletes data processed by the function.
steps.outcome: Gained the ability to destructively modify or delete sensitive data processed by the Lambda function.

mitigation.description: Implement continuous monitoring and validation of code integrity to detect unauthorized changes to Lambda functions or other application components.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Lambda Code Signing or third-party tools to monitor and validate the integrity of Lambda function code.

steps.action_description: Leverage the permissions granted to the Lambda role to manipulate user session data stored in DynamoDB, potentially enabling persistence or further lateral movement.
steps.outcome: Gained the ability to manipulate user session data, potentially enabling persistence or further lateral movement within the environment.

mitigation.description: Implement strict access controls and monitoring mechanisms to detect and prevent unauthorized access or modifications to sensitive data stores.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Enable AWS CloudTrail and configure appropriate logging and monitoring for DynamoDB and other critical data stores.

steps.action_description: Abuse the permissions granted to the Lambda role to deface or modify the static assets hosted in the S3 bucket, potentially for further malicious purposes.
steps.outcome: Successfully defaced or modified the static assets hosted in the S3 bucket, potentially causing disruption or further malicious activities.

mitigation.description: Implement secure practices for hosting and serving static content, including access controls, content validation, and versioning.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CloudFront or AWS WAF to secure and protect static content hosted in S3 buckets.

attack_paths.name: Compromised Web Server to Database Access
attack_paths.objective: Gain unauthorized access to the database containing sensitive user data

steps.action_description: Scan the internal network from the compromised web server to discover open ports and services.
steps.outcome: Identified the database server and its open port.

mitigation.description: Implement network segmentation to restrict access between different network zones.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Network Firewall to create stateful rules and restrict traffic between subnets.

steps.action_description: Leverage the IAM instance profile attached to the web server to obtain temporary AWS credentials.
steps.outcome: Obtained temporary AWS credentials with permissions to access other AWS resources.

mitigation.description: Apply the principle of least privilege and restrict access to only necessary resources.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Review and tighten the IAM policies attached to the instance profile, limiting access to only required services and resources.

steps.action_description: Use the obtained AWS credentials to create a temporary database connection and hijack an existing session.
steps.outcome: Gained unauthorized access to the database server and its contents.

mitigation.description: Enable AWS CloudTrail to log and monitor AWS service activity, including database connections.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Configure CloudTrail to capture and log database connection events for auditing and detection purposes.

attack_paths.name: Compromised Lambda Function to S3 Data Exfiltration
attack_paths.objective: Exfiltrate sensitive data from S3 buckets

steps.action_description: Scan the internal network from the compromised Lambda function to discover accessible resources.
steps.outcome: Identified S3 buckets containing sensitive data.

mitigation.description: Implement network segmentation to restrict access between different network zones.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Network Firewall to create stateful rules and restrict traffic between subnets.

steps.action_description: Leverage the IAM role attached to the Lambda function to obtain temporary AWS credentials.
steps.outcome: Obtained temporary AWS credentials with permissions to access S3 buckets.

mitigation.description: Apply the principle of least privilege and restrict access to only necessary resources.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Review and tighten the IAM policies attached to the Lambda role, limiting access to only required services and resources.

steps.action_description: Use the obtained AWS credentials to access and exfiltrate data from the S3 buckets.
steps.outcome: Sensitive user data and documents were exfiltrated from the S3 bucket.

mitigation.description: Implement network intrusion prevention systems to detect and prevent unauthorized data exfiltration.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Deploy AWS Network Firewall with rules to detect and block unauthorized S3 bucket access and data exfiltration attempts.

attack_paths.name: Compromised EC2 Instance to Privilege Escalation
attack_paths.objective: Escalate privileges and gain administrative access

steps.action_description: Scan the internal network from the compromised web server to discover accessible resources and services.
steps.outcome: Identified the AWS Secrets Manager service and its associated secrets.

mitigation.description: Implement network segmentation to restrict access between different network zones.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Network Firewall to create stateful rules and restrict traffic between subnets.

steps.action_description: Leverage the IAM instance profile attached to the web server to obtain temporary AWS credentials.
steps.outcome: Obtained temporary AWS credentials with permissions to access AWS Secrets Manager.

mitigation.description: Apply the principle of least privilege and restrict access to only necessary resources.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Review and tighten the IAM policies attached to the instance profile, limiting access to only required services and resources.

steps.action_description: Use the obtained AWS credentials to access and retrieve sensitive API keys stored in AWS Secrets Manager.
steps.outcome: Gained access to privileged API keys, potentially allowing further privilege escalation and unauthorized access.

mitigation.description: Enable AWS CloudTrail to log and monitor AWS service activity, including access to Secrets Manager.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Configure CloudTrail to capture and log Secrets Manager access events for auditing and detection purposes.

attack_paths.name: Exfiltrate User Documents from S3
attack_paths.objective: Data Exfiltration

steps.action_description: Discover the existence of the user_documents S3 bucket containing sensitive user data.
steps.outcome: Identified high-value target for data exfiltration.

mitigation.mitigation_name: Application Removal of File Viewer Utilities
mitigation.description: Remove or disable unnecessary file viewing utilities that can be used to exfiltrate data.
aws_service_action: Audit and remove unnecessary file viewing utilities

steps.action_description: Obtain valid AWS credentials from the web server instance profile.
steps.outcome: Gained access to AWS resources with permissions granted to the web_server_role.

mitigation.description: Implement privileged account management principles, including least privilege and separation of duties.

steps.action_description: Use obtained AWS credentials to access and copy data from the user_documents S3 bucket.
steps.outcome: Sensitive user data exfiltrated from the S3 bucket.

mitigation.description: Implement data obfuscation techniques to protect sensitive data at rest.
aws_service_action: Implement AWS KMS for encryption key management

attack_paths.name: Exfiltrate Secrets from Secrets Manager
attack_paths.objective: Data Exfiltration

steps.action_description: Discover the existence of the api_keys secret in AWS Secrets Manager.
steps.outcome: Identified high-value target for data exfiltration.

mitigation.mitigation_name: Application Removal of File Viewer Utilities
mitigation.description: Remove or disable unnecessary file viewing utilities that can be used to exfiltrate data.
aws_service_action: Audit and remove unnecessary file viewing utilities

steps.action_description: Obtain valid AWS credentials from the lambda_role.
steps.outcome: Gained access to AWS resources with permissions granted to the lambda_role.

mitigation.description: Implement privileged account management principles, including least privilege and separation of duties.

steps.action_description: Use obtained AWS credentials to access and exfiltrate the api_keys secret via AWS API calls.
steps.outcome: Sensitive API keys exfiltrated from AWS Secrets Manager.

mitigation.description: Implement data obfuscation techniques to protect sensitive data at rest.
aws_service_action: Enable AWS Secrets Manager encryption at rest
Implement AWS KMS for encryption key management

steps.action_description: Establish a command-and-control channel to exfiltrate the stolen API keys.
steps.outcome: Sensitive API keys exfiltrated from the environment.

mitigation.description: Implement network intrusion prevention systems to detect and prevent unauthorized data exfiltration.
aws_service_action: Configure AWS WAF for web application protection

attack_paths.name: Exfiltrate User Session Data from DynamoDB
attack_paths.objective: Data Exfiltration

steps.action_description: Discover the existence of the user_sessions DynamoDB table containing sensitive user session data.
steps.outcome: Identified high-value target for data exfiltration.

mitigation.mitigation_name: Application Removal of File Viewer Utilities
mitigation.description: Remove or disable unnecessary file viewing utilities that can be used to exfiltrate data.
aws_service_action: Audit and remove unnecessary file viewing utilities

steps.action_description: Obtain valid AWS credentials from the web server instance profile.
steps.outcome: Gained access to AWS resources with permissions granted to the web_server_role.

mitigation.description: Implement privileged account management principles, including least privilege and separation of duties.

steps.action_description: Use obtained AWS credentials to access and copy data from the user_sessions DynamoDB table.
steps.outcome: Sensitive user session data exfiltrated from the DynamoDB table.

mitigation.description: Implement data obfuscation techniques to protect sensitive data at rest.
aws_service_action: Enable AWS DynamoDB encryption at rest
Implement AWS KMS for encryption key management

steps.action_description: Automate the exfiltration process to continuously extract user session data from the DynamoDB table.
steps.outcome: Sensitive user session data continuously exfiltrated from the environment.

mitigation.description: Implement endpoint security solutions to detect and prevent unauthorized data exfiltration.
aws_service_action: Deploy AWS Systems Manager for endpoint management
Configure AWS Security Hub for threat detection

attack_paths.name: Web Server Instance Metadata Exploitation
attack_paths.objective: Obtain privileged access to web server instances
attack_paths.threat_actor: Cloud Infrastructure and IAM Exploitation Specialist

steps.action_description: Enumerate the Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) to obtain temporary AWS credentials associated with the web server instance profile
steps.outcome: Obtained temporary AWS credentials with permissions granted to the web server role

mitigation.description: Disable the Instance Metadata Service on the web server instances to prevent unauthorized access to instance metadata
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws:ec2:disableInstanceMetadataService

steps.action_description: Use the obtained temporary AWS credentials to assume the web server role and access resources granted to the role
steps.outcome: Gained access to resources and permissions granted to the web server role

mitigation.description: Implement least privilege principles and regularly review and update IAM roles and policies to minimize the impact of compromised credentials
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws:iam:updateRolePolicy

steps.action_description: Use the assumed web server role privileges to access and exfiltrate sensitive user documents stored in the S3 bucket
steps.outcome: Obtained sensitive user data stored in the S3 bucket

mitigation.description: Implement strict access controls and least privilege principles for the S3 bucket containing sensitive user documents
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws:s3:putBucketPolicy

attack_paths.name: Serverless Function Privilege Escalation
attack_paths.objective: Escalate privileges and access sensitive data
attack_paths.threat_actor: Cloud Infrastructure and IAM Exploitation Specialist

steps.action_description: Enumerate the Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) to obtain temporary AWS credentials associated with the web server instance profile
steps.outcome: Obtained temporary AWS credentials with permissions granted to the web server role

mitigation.description: Disable the Instance Metadata Service on the web server instances to prevent unauthorized access to instance metadata
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws:ec2:disableInstanceMetadataService

steps.action_description: Use the obtained temporary AWS credentials to assume the web server role and access resources granted to the role
steps.outcome: Gained access to resources and permissions granted to the web server role

mitigation.description: Implement least privilege principles and regularly review and update IAM roles and policies to minimize the impact of compromised credentials
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws:iam:updateRolePolicy

steps.action_description: Use the assumed web server role privileges to access and exfiltrate sensitive API keys stored in AWS Secrets Manager
steps.outcome: Obtained sensitive API keys that could be used for further privilege escalation

mitigation.description: Implement strict access controls and least privilege principles for AWS Secrets Manager to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive secrets
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws:secretsmanager:putResourcePolicy

steps.action_description: Use the obtained API keys to invoke the document_processor Lambda function with elevated privileges
steps.outcome: Gained elevated privileges and access to resources granted to the Lambda function role

mitigation.description: Implement secure Lambda function execution by restricting access to sensitive resources and enforcing least privilege principles
mitigation.aws_service_action: aws:lambda:updateFunctionConfiguration

attack_paths.name: Compromised Third-Party Library
attack_paths.objective: Gain unauthorized access to web servers

steps.action_description: Identify third-party libraries used by the web application running on the web servers.
steps.outcome: Discovered a vulnerable third-party library used by the web application.

mitigation.description: Use software inventory tools to track and monitor third-party libraries and their versions.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CodeBuild or AWS CodePipeline to integrate software inventory tools into the build process.

steps.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
steps.action_description: Compromise the vulnerable third-party library by injecting malicious code.
steps.outcome: Compromised third-party library with malicious code.

mitigation.description: Implement software integrity checks to verify the authenticity and integrity of third-party libraries before use.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CodeArtifact or AWS CodeCommit to host and verify third-party libraries.

steps.action_description: Deploy the compromised third-party library to the web servers during the application update process.
steps.outcome: Compromised third-party library deployed on web servers.

mitigation.description: Isolate and sandbox the web application to limit the impact of a compromised third-party library.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Fargate or AWS ECS to run the web application in isolated containers.

steps.action_description: Leverage the compromised third-party library to gain unauthorized access to the web server's IAM instance profile.
steps.outcome: Gained unauthorized access to the web server's IAM instance profile.

mitigation.description: Implement privileged account management to restrict and monitor access to sensitive IAM roles and instance profiles.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to manage and monitor access to IAM roles and instance profiles.

attack_paths.name: Compromised Container Image
attack_paths.objective: Gain unauthorized access to Lambda function

steps.action_description: Identify the container image used to build the Lambda function.
steps.outcome: Discovered a publicly available container image used to build the Lambda function.

mitigation.description: Restrict access to public container registries and only allow trusted container images.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS ECR to host and manage container images, and restrict access to public registries.

steps.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
steps.action_description: Compromise the container image by injecting malicious code.
steps.outcome: Compromised container image with malicious code.

mitigation.description: Implement secure configuration management for container images, including vulnerability scanning and code signing.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS ECR Image Scanning to scan container images for vulnerabilities, and use AWS CodeArtifact to manage and sign container images.

steps.action_description: Deploy the compromised container image to the Lambda function during an update.
steps.outcome: Compromised container image deployed to the Lambda function.

mitigation.description: Use a trusted container repository to store and manage container images.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS ECR as a trusted container repository and enforce image signing and verification.

steps.action_description: Leverage the compromised container image to gain unauthorized access to the Lambda function's IAM role.
steps.outcome: Gained unauthorized access to the Lambda function's IAM role.

mitigation.description: Implement privileged account management to restrict and monitor access to sensitive IAM roles.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to manage and monitor access to IAM roles.

attack_paths.name: Compromised CI/CD Pipeline
attack_paths.objective: Gain unauthorized access to application resources

steps.action_description: Identify the CI/CD pipeline used for deploying the web application.
steps.outcome: Discovered a vulnerable CI/CD pipeline used for deploying the web application.

mitigation.description: Restrict the ability to install or modify software on systems involved in the CI/CD pipeline.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CodeBuild or AWS CodePipeline with strict permissions and access controls.

steps.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
steps.action_description: Compromise the CI/CD pipeline by injecting malicious code or modifying deployment scripts.
steps.outcome: Compromised CI/CD pipeline with malicious code or modified deployment scripts.

mitigation.description: Implement secure configuration management for the CI/CD pipeline, including code signing and verification.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CodeArtifact to manage and sign deployment artifacts, and AWS CodePipeline to enforce artifact verification.

steps.action_description: Trigger a deployment through the compromised CI/CD pipeline, delivering malicious code or modified deployment scripts.
steps.outcome: Malicious code or modified deployment scripts delivered to the web servers.

mitigation.description: Implement auditing and logging for the CI/CD pipeline to detect and investigate potential compromises.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CloudTrail and AWS Config to monitor and audit changes to the CI/CD pipeline.

steps.action_description: Leverage the compromised deployment to gain unauthorized access to the web server's IAM instance profile.
steps.outcome: Gained unauthorized access to the web server's IAM instance profile.

mitigation.description: Implement privileged account management to restrict and monitor access to sensitive IAM roles and instance profiles.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to manage and monitor access to IAM roles and instance profiles.

attack_paths.name: Scattered Spider MFA Fatigue Attack
attack_paths.objective: Obtain privileged cloud console access

steps.action_description: Send phishing emails to web server administrators to obtain initial credentials
steps.outcome: Obtained valid user credentials

mitigation.description: Train users to identify and report phishing attempts.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Implement security awareness training for employees

steps.action_description: Use stolen credentials to log into the web server instance
steps.outcome: Gained initial foothold on web server

mitigation.description: Require multi-factor authentication for all cloud accounts and instances.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Enable AWS Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) and configure it as a requirement

steps.action_description: Discover IAM instance profile and associated role permissions
steps.outcome: Identified privileged IAM role with access to Secrets Manager

mitigation.description: Implement least privilege principles and review permissions regularly.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to apply least privilege permissions

steps.action_description: Use instance profile credentials to retrieve secrets from AWS Secrets Manager
steps.outcome: Obtained sensitive API keys and cloud credentials

mitigation.description: Monitor and detect suspicious access to cloud service credentials.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Enable AWS CloudTrail and configure monitoring for Secrets Manager access

steps.action_description: Use stolen credentials to initiate MFA fatigue/push bombing attack against privileged IAM role
steps.outcome: Gained control of privileged IAM role with Lambda access

mitigation.description: Implement policies to prevent MFA fatigue attacks by limiting login attempts.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Configure AWS account settings to block accounts after a certain number of failed MFA attempts

attack_paths.name: Scattered Spider Help Desk Impersonation
attack_paths.objective: Obtain privileged cloud console access

steps.action_description: Send phishing emails to web server administrators to gather information about IT support processes
steps.outcome: Obtained details about the organization's IT help desk procedures

mitigation.description: Train users to identify and report phishing attempts.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Implement security awareness training for employees

steps.action_description: Use stolen credentials or compromised accounts to gain initial access to the web server
steps.outcome: Gained initial foothold on web server

mitigation.description: Require multi-factor authentication for all cloud accounts and instances.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Enable AWS Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) and configure it as a requirement

steps.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
steps.action_description: Disable or tamper with application logs to cover tracks
steps.outcome: Evaded detection by disabling logging mechanisms

mitigation.description: Ensure comprehensive auditing and logging of all actions and events.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Enable AWS CloudTrail and configure centralized logging with CloudWatch

steps.action_description: Impersonate IT help desk and socially engineer cloud administrators to escalate privileges
steps.outcome: Obtained privileged IAM role credentials with Lambda access

mitigation.description: Monitor and alert on suspicious authentication activities.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Implement AWS CloudTrail monitoring and alerting for IAM role escalations

steps.action_description: Use hijacked IAM role to deploy malicious Lambda function for data exfiltration
steps.outcome: Gained ability to exfiltrate sensitive data from the cloud environment

mitigation.description: Continuously monitor cloud services for unauthorized or suspicious activities.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Implement AWS Config rules and CloudWatch Events to detect unauthorized Lambda deployments

attack_paths.name: Web Application Data Exfiltration
attack_paths.objective: Exfiltrate sensitive user data and payment information

steps.action_description: Conduct reconnaissance and identify vulnerabilities in the public-facing web application load balancer
steps.outcome: Gained initial foothold on the web server

mitigation.description: Perform regular vulnerability scanning and patching of the web application and infrastructure components.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Inspector or third-party tools to scan for vulnerabilities.

steps.action_description: Use PowerShell scripts to escalate privileges and move laterally within the web server
steps.outcome: Gained administrative access to the web server

mitigation.description: Implement application control policies and restrict execution of unauthorized code, including PowerShell scripts.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Systems Manager to manage and restrict code execution on EC2 instances.

steps.action_description: Inject malicious code into legitimate web server processes to access sensitive data
steps.outcome: Accessed user session data and payment information

mitigation.description: Implement application isolation and sandboxing techniques to prevent process injection and code execution.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Fargate or AWS Lambda to run applications in isolated environments.

steps.action_description: Establish a command and control channel to exfiltrate the stolen data
steps.outcome: Sensitive user data and payment information exfiltrated

mitigation.description: Monitor and analyze outbound network traffic for unauthorized data exfiltration.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Network Firewall or third-party security solutions to monitor and control outbound traffic.

attack_paths.name: Serverless Function Data Manipulation
attack_paths.objective: Manipulate serverless functions to access and tamper with user data

steps.action_description: Analyze the serverless function code and identify potential data exfiltration vectors
steps.outcome: Identified potential vulnerabilities in the serverless function

mitigation.description: Perform regular vulnerability scanning and testing of serverless functions and applications.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Lambda Layers or third-party tools to scan and test Lambda functions.

steps.action_description: Deploy a malicious web shell to the serverless function to gain remote code execution
steps.outcome: Gained remote code execution capability on the serverless function

mitigation.description: Restrict access to cloud metadata and instance metadata services to prevent unauthorized access and code execution.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies and resource-based policies to restrict access to metadata services.

steps.action_description: Use the compromised serverless function to inject malicious links into user session data
steps.outcome: Injected malicious links into user session data

mitigation.description: Implement data integrity monitoring to detect and prevent unauthorized modifications to sensitive data stores.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CloudTrail and AWS Config to monitor and audit changes to data stores like DynamoDB.

steps.action_description: Stage the compromised user session data in the application logs bucket for exfiltration
steps.outcome: Sensitive user session data staged for exfiltration

mitigation.description: Implement network segmentation to isolate critical systems and data stores from potential threats.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS VPC and security groups to segment and isolate resources based on their sensitivity and trust levels.

attack_paths.name: Backdoor Web Servers via Supply Chain Compromise

steps.outcome: Identified IP addresses of web servers behind the load balancer

mitigation.description: Deploy network monitoring sensors to detect active scanning activities

steps.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
steps.action_description: Compromise upstream software vendor and inject backdoor into web application

mitigation.description: Implement secure software development practices and verify software integrity
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CodeArtifact to host and verify software artifacts

steps.action_description: Exploit backdoor in web application to gain initial access to web servers
steps.outcome: Achieved code execution on web servers

mitigation.description: Implement application sandboxing and isolation techniques

steps.action_description: Modify web server instances to maintain persistent backdoor access
steps.outcome: Established persistent backdoor access to web servers

steps.action_description: Compromise the Lambda execution role to gain access to its permissions
steps.outcome: Obtained valid credentials for the Lambda execution role

steps.outcome: Exfiltrated sensitive user documents from S3 bucket

mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Lambda Layers to isolate Lambda functions

steps.outcome: Exfiltrated sensitive user session data from DynamoDB table

mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS DynamoDB Encryption to encrypt data at rest

steps.outcome: Achieved code execution on web server instance

mitigation.description: Implement application sandboxing and isolation techniques

steps.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API

steps.outcome: Obtained IAM credentials with permissions to access other resources

mitigation.description: Restrict access to the Instance Metadata Service

attack_paths.name: Persistent Access via Cloud Workload
attack_paths.objective: Gain persistent access to the cloud environment

steps.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
steps.action_description: Compromise the software supply chain for the web server instances by injecting malicious code into third-party dependencies or development tools used in the build process.
steps.outcome: Establish initial foothold on web server instances.

mitigation.description: Implement secure software development practices, including verifying the integrity of third-party dependencies and development tools, and using trusted sources.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CodePipeline, AWS CodeBuild, and AWS CodeDeploy with secure configurations to manage the software build and deployment process.

steps.action_description: Leverage the compromised web server instances to obtain the AWS credentials associated with the web_server_role IAM instance profile.
steps.outcome: Gain elevated privileges within the AWS environment.

mitigation.description: Implement least privilege principles and strictly manage access to privileged accounts and roles.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to enforce least privilege access and regularly review and rotate credentials.

steps.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
steps.action_description: Use the compromised AWS credentials to obtain temporary access tokens or assume roles with permissions to access the document_processor Lambda function.
steps.outcome: Gain access to the serverless Lambda function and its associated resources.

mitigation.description: Restrict access to credentials and implement strict access controls and monitoring for credential usage.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies and AWS CloudTrail to monitor and restrict access to AWS credentials and roles.

steps.action_description: Use the compromised Lambda function to access and exfiltrate sensitive user documents stored in the S3 bucket.
steps.outcome: Gain access to sensitive user data for intelligence collection or further exploitation.

mitigation.description: Implement strict access controls and monitoring for data access, including encryption at rest and in transit.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies, AWS Key Management Service (KMS), and AWS CloudTrail to restrict and monitor access to sensitive data in S3 buckets.

steps.action_description: Use the compromised Lambda function to access and modify user session data stored in the DynamoDB table, potentially enabling persistence and command and control capabilities.
steps.outcome: Establish a persistent command and control channel within the cloud environment.

mitigation.description: Implement strict access controls and monitoring for data access, including encryption at rest and in transit.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies, AWS Key Management Service (KMS), and AWS CloudTrail to restrict and monitor access to sensitive data in DynamoDB tables.

attack_paths.name: Cloud Service Exploitation
attack_paths.objective: Exploit cloud services for persistent access and data exfiltration

steps.action_description: Exploit a vulnerability in the public-facing web application hosted on the web server instances to gain initial access.
steps.outcome: Establish initial foothold on the web server instances.

mitigation.description: Implement a comprehensive vulnerability management program, including regular patching and security updates.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager to automate patching of EC2 instances and AWS Inspector to scan for vulnerabilities.

steps.action_description: Leverage the compromised web server instances to obtain the AWS credentials associated with the web_server_role IAM instance profile.
steps.outcome: Gain elevated privileges within the AWS environment.

mitigation.description: Implement least privilege principles and strictly manage access to privileged accounts and roles.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to enforce least privilege access and regularly review and rotate credentials.

steps.action_description: Use the compromised web server instances to launch a spearphishing campaign targeting AWS administrators, tricking them into exposing their credentials or approving malicious OAuth applications.
steps.outcome: Obtain additional privileged AWS credentials or roles, enabling lateral movement within the cloud environment.

mitigation.description: Implement regular security awareness training for employees, including identifying and reporting phishing attempts.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Security Hub and AWS GuardDuty to detect and respond to potential phishing attacks and compromised credentials.

steps.action_description: Use the compromised AWS credentials to access and exfiltrate sensitive API keys and secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager.
steps.outcome: Gain access to sensitive data and potentially enable further exploitation of cloud resources.

mitigation.description: Implement strict access controls and monitoring for data access, including encryption at rest and in transit.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies, AWS Key Management Service (KMS), and AWS CloudTrail to restrict and monitor access to sensitive data in AWS Secrets Manager.

steps.action_description: Leverage the compromised AWS credentials to deploy malicious software or backdoors to the web server instances, potentially enabling persistent command and control capabilities.
steps.outcome: Establish a persistent command and control channel within the cloud environment.

mitigation.description: Implement secure software deployment practices, including verifying the integrity of deployed software and using trusted sources.
mitigation.aws_service_action: Use AWS CodePipeline, AWS CodeBuild, and AWS CodeDeploy with secure configurations to manage the software build and deployment process.

attack_paths.name: Cryptocurrency Theft and Destructive Attack
attack_paths.objective: Steal cryptocurrency assets and deploy destructive wiper malware

steps.action_description: Compromise the web server instance through a watering hole attack, exploiting a vulnerability in the web application.
steps.outcome: Establish initial foothold on the web server

mitigation.description: Deploy web application firewalls, keep software updated, and implement secure coding practices to prevent exploitation.
mitigation.aws_service_action: AWS WAF, AWS Shield, AWS Inspector

steps.action_description: Enumerate the IAM permissions associated with the web server instance profile to discover access to other AWS services.
steps.outcome: Discover access to S3 buckets and AWS Secrets Manager

mitigation.description: Implement least privilege principles and regularly review and rotate IAM credentials and permissions.
mitigation.aws_service_action: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)

steps.action_description: Use the discovered IAM permissions to access and exfiltrate sensitive user documents stored in the S3 bucket.
steps.outcome: Steal sensitive user data and financial records

mitigation.description: Implement data encryption at rest and in transit for sensitive data stored in S3 buckets.
mitigation.aws_service_action: AWS Key Management Service (KMS), AWS S3 Server-Side Encryption

steps.action_description: Deploy a destructive wiper malware that encrypts and renders the database instance unusable.
steps.outcome: Deny access to the database and disrupt application functionality

mitigation.description: Implement regular backups of the database instance to enable recovery in case of destructive attacks.
mitigation.aws_service_action: AWS Backup, AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)
